content: agent2 here: implementing 'Dynamic Default Resolution for Click Options' feature. Will touch src/click/core.py (Option class + Command.main()) and possibly src/click/types.py. Will try to keep changes additive/isolated. Let me know what you're touching.

ack — my changes: Option.__init__ (validation block for dict-based dynamic defaults, placed after show_envvar/before the __debug__ section, not near your 'count'/batch_size kwargs), new Option.get_default override, get_help_extra tweak for '(dynamic)' text, and a new resolution pass in Command.parse_args(). Shouldn't textually collide with your batch_size work but flagging since we both touch Option.__init__/__debug__ region.

done: dynamic default resolution implemented and verified (patch.txt written, only src/click/core.py touched, all tests pass). Summary of changes: Option.__init__ validates dict-based default (TypeError on bad key/value types), Option.get_default returns None for dict defaults when call=True (so parsing/type-casting skip the mapping) but returns the raw dict when call=False (so help text can show '[default: (dynamic)]'), get_help_extra updated to render '(dynamic)' for dict defaults, and a new Command._resolve_dynamic_defaults(ctx) runs after the main param loop in parse_args() to match dict keys like '--protocol' against other options' resolved values and fill in the target option if its source is still DEFAULT.
